# Skill: HTML Engineer

## Purpose
Generate a properly separated, multi-file website from the confirmed Site Map, Section Blueprints, Design Token Set, and Image Assignment Map. Output is never a single self-contained file — CSS, JS, HTML pages, and partials are always separate files.

---

## § Scaffolding

Before writing any content, create the full output folder structure:

```
output/
├── index.html
├── [page-name].html          ← one file per page in the Site Map
├── scripts/
│   └── compile_includes.py   ← compiles include markers into runtime pages
├── partials/
│   ├── header.html           ← generated by partials-manager.md
│   └── footer.html           ← generated by partials-manager.md
└── assets/
    ├── css/
    │   └── main.css
    ├── js/
    │   └── main.js
    └── images/               ← symlink or copy from knowledge-files/assets/images/
```

Create all folders and empty placeholder files first. Confirm the structure to the user before writing any content.

After page generation, run include compilation:

```bash
python scripts/compile_includes.py
```

This resolves `<!-- include: ... -->` markers into full runtime HTML so nav/footer render when opening or serving pages.

---

## § CSS

Generate `output/assets/css/main.css` as a single shared stylesheet. Structure it in this order:

### 1. Design Tokens
```css
:root {
  /* Colours */
  --bg-primary:    [hex];
  --bg-secondary:  [hex];
  --accent:        [hex];
  --accent-dark:   [hex];
  --text-primary:  [hex];
  --text-muted:    [hex];
  --border:        [rgba or hex];
  --white:         [hex];

  /* Typography */
  --font-body:     [stack];
  --font-heading:  [stack];
  --font-mono:     [stack, if used];

  /* Spacing */
  --space-section: [px];
  --space-card:    [px];
  --space-gap:     [px];

  /* Shape */
  --radius-card:   [px];
  --radius-btn:    [px];

  /* Animation */
  --transition:    [e.g. 0.25s ease];
}
```

### 2. Reset & Base
Minimal reset: box-sizing, margin/padding zero, font inheritance. Base body styles using tokens only.

### 3. Typography Scale
`h1` through `h4`, `p`, `a`, `small` — all using `var()` tokens. No hardcoded values.

### 4. Layout Utilities
Reusable classes: `.container`, `.grid`, `.flex`, `.section-padding`. These are used by all pages.

### 5. Component Styles
One block per component from `component-library.md`. Each block is clearly commented:
```css
/* ── Hero ── */
/* ── Nav ── */
/* ── Cards ── */
/* ── Footer ── */
```

### 6. Responsive Overrides
Mobile-first. Breakpoints at the end of the file:
```css
@media (min-width: 768px)  { ... }
@media (min-width: 1100px) { ... }
```

### Rules
- Every value must use a `var(--token)` — no hardcoded hex, px, or font names anywhere in the file
- No `!important`
- No page-specific styles in `main.css` — if a page needs unique styles, create `assets/css/[page-name].css` and link it only from that page

---

## § JS

Generate `output/assets/js/main.js` as a single shared script. Structure it in this order:

### 1. File Header Comment
```js
// main.js — Website Builder Harness
// Shared interactive behaviour for all pages.
// No frameworks. No inline handlers. addEventListener only.
```

### 2. DOM-Ready Wrapper
All code runs inside:
```js
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  // all initialisation here
});
```

### 3. Component Modules
One clearly commented block per interactive component:
```js
// ── Mobile Nav Toggle ──
// ── FAQ Accordion ──
// ── Marquee ──
// ── Scroll Animations ──
```

### 4. Init Calls
Each module is a function. Call all init functions at the bottom of the DOM-ready wrapper.

### Rules
- Vanilla JS only — no frameworks or libraries
- No `onclick`, `onload`, or any inline event handlers in HTML files
- All selectors use `data-` attributes or semantic class names — never IDs for JS hooks
- If a behaviour is page-specific, create `assets/js/[page-name].js` and link it only from that page
- Use `defer` on all `<script>` tags in HTML

---

## § Pages

Generate one `.html` file per page in the Site Map. Every page file follows this exact structure:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>[Page Title] — [Site Name]</title>
  <meta name="description" content="[Page-specific description]" />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/main.css" />
  <!-- Page-specific CSS only if needed -->
  <!-- <link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/[page-name].css" /> -->
</head>
<body>

  <!-- HEADER PARTIAL -->
  <!-- include: partials/header.html -->

  <main>
    <!-- Page sections go here, using component classes from main.css -->
  </main>

  <!-- FOOTER PARTIAL -->
  <!-- include: partials/footer.html -->

  <script src="assets/js/main.js" defer></script>
  <!-- Page-specific JS only if needed -->
  <!-- <script src="assets/js/[page-name].js" defer></script> -->
</body>
</html>
```

### Minimum Required HTML Per Section

Every section in the Section Blueprint **must** be rendered as a fully
structured HTML block. Bare text output — content without wrapping HTML
elements and component classes — is a validation failure and must be rejected.

Each section must follow this minimum structure:

    <section class="[component-class] section-padding" id="[section-id]">
      <div class="container">
        <!-- section content here using component classes from main.css -->
      </div>
    </section>

The `[component-class]` must match a class defined in `component-library.md`
and present in `main.css`. If the class does not exist in `main.css`, add it
before generating the page.

**Common failure patterns to avoid:**

| ❌ Wrong — bare text        | ✅ Correct — structured HTML                              |
|---|---|
| `Feel Great Every Time`     | `<h1 class="hero__title">Feel Great Every Time</h1>`     |
| `Book Now`                  | `<a href="#" class="btn btn--primary">Book Now</a>`      |
| `Hair Services`             | `<div class="card"><h3>Hair Services</h3>...</div>`      |
| `$200`                      | `<p class="package__price">$200</p>`                     |

Every piece of content must be wrapped in a semantic HTML element with an
appropriate class before the page is considered complete.


### Partial Include Convention
Use include comment markers as source templates during generation:
```html
<!-- include: partials/header.html -->
```

Then compile these markers into runtime HTML using:

```bash
python scripts/compile_includes.py
```

The agent must output `partials/header.html` and `partials/footer.html` as complete source partials and must compile page files before final delivery.

### Build and Preview
From the project root:

```bash
python scripts/compile_includes.py
python -m http.server 8000
```

Open:

```text
http://localhost:8000/output/index.html
```

### Page-Specific Rules
- Each page has its own `<title>` and `<meta name="description">`
- The `<h1>` on each page reflects that page's primary topic — not the site name
- Active nav link must be marked with `aria-current="page"` and a CSS `.active` class
- Relative paths for CSS, JS, and images must be correct for each page's depth in the folder structure

### Image Rules
- Use the exact `File path` from the Image Assignment Map as the `src` — adjusted to be relative to the page's location
- Use the exact `Alt text` from the metadata verbatim
- Use `loading="lazy"` on all images except the hero
- Hero background images use CSS `background-image` in `main.css`, not an `<img>` tag
- `.avif` images must be wrapped in `<picture>` with a `.jpg` fallback if one exists in the metadata
- Never use the same image twice on the same page

---

## § Validation

Run this checklist across all output files before delivering.

### Site Structure
- [ ] Every page in the Site Map has a corresponding `.html` file in `output/`
- [ ] `output/assets/css/main.css` exists and contains all tokens
- [ ] `output/assets/js/main.js` exists
- [ ] `output/partials/header.html` exists
- [ ] `output/partials/footer.html` exists
- [ ] `scripts/compile_includes.py` exists
- [ ] No `<style>` blocks exist in any `.html` file
- [ ] No `<script>` blocks or inline handlers exist in any `.html` file
- [ ] No unresolved include markers remain in runtime pages under `output/*.html`

### Per-Page
- [ ] Every section in that page's Section Blueprint is present
- [ ] No section added that wasn't in the blueprint
- [ ] Primary CTA appears in the hero and at least one other section
- [ ] All content sourced from the knowledge file — nothing invented
- [ ] Single `<h1>` per page
- [ ] Active nav link has `aria-current="page"`
- [ ] `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` are unique per page
- [ ] `<header class="site-header">` is present in each runtime page
- [ ] `<footer class="site-footer">` is present in each runtime page

### CSS
- [ ] All values use `var(--token)` — no hardcoded hex, px, or font names
- [ ] Full token set declared in `:root`
- [ ] No `!important`
- [ ] Mobile layout correct at 375px

### JS
- [ ] All behaviour in `main.js` or page-specific JS files
- [ ] No inline event handlers in any HTML file
- [ ] All `<script>` tags use `defer`

### Images
- [ ] Every `src` matches the exact file path from `image-metadata.txt`
- [ ] Every `alt` matches the exact alt text from metadata
- [ ] All non-hero images have `loading="lazy"`
- [ ] `.avif` images wrapped in `<picture>` with fallback
- [ ] No image used more than once per page

### Accessibility
- [ ] All `<img>` tags have non-empty `alt`
- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible
- [ ] Visible focus states on all interactive elements
- [ ] `aria-label` on all icon-only buttons
